meaning: n.热带(或亚热带)稀树大草原
examples: An adult male lion is resting in the grass on the Kenyan savanna.
The herders shifted south, following the major river systems into savanna regions.
Increase in pastureland and subsequent overgrazing have resulted in an expansion of savanna.
Forest elephants are distinguished from the more familiar savanna elephants by their smaller size and straighter tusks.
And the implication is that what is currently savanna will become forest: we might assume that what is currently grasslands will become savanna as well.
